name: project-steward
description: Organize local, SSH, and Dev Container projects in a synchronized project steward.

commands.title: Project Steward: Add Projects from Folder
Project Steward: Add File to Active Terminal

projectSteward.projectData.markdownDescription: Stores the Project Steward data if the ```projectSteward.storeProjectsInSettings``` option is set to ```true```. The raw data can be edited by using the command ```Project Steward: Edit Projects```.

projectSteward.todoData.markdownDescription: Stores the global Project Steward TODO list if the ```projectSteward.storeProjectsInSettings``` option is set to ```true```.

projectSteward.customCss.markdownDescription: Custom CSS rules to be injected into the Project Steward can be placed here. Note that this will not be sanitized!

projectSteward.recentColors.markdownDescription: Stores the recently used colors if the ```projectSteward.storeProjectsInSettings``` option is set to ```true```.

projectSteward.storeProjectsInSettings.markdownDescription: If set to ```true```, the Project Steward data is saved in the user settings and can be synced using the ```Settings Sync``` extension to multiple devices. NOTE: This disables the possibility of having varying Project Steward data on these devices. **(VSCode restart required)**

projectSteward.maxVisibleAiSessions.markdownDescription: Maximum number of AI session rows visible in an expanded project card before the session list scrolls.

projectSteward.aiSessionTerminalMode.description: Choose whether new AI sessions run in VS Code terminals or managed tmux sessions. tmux preserves a process only while its execution host remains awake and running.

enumDescriptions: Group AI sessions as windows in one managed tmux session per project.
Run every AI session in its own managed tmux session.
projectSteward.aiSessionTmuxLayout.description: Choose how managed tmux sessions are organized.

projectSteward.aiSessionTmuxPath.description: Executable name resolved through the current extension host's PATH, or an absolute executable path. Enter one executable only, without additional tmux arguments or a shell command.

enumDescriptions: Electric currents flow along the top and bottom card edges.
A diagonal light beam sweeps across the card periodically.
A glowing dot with a comet tail orbits along the card edge.
A rotating glowing halo surrounds the project kind icon.
Sonar ripples expand from the project kind icon.
The whole card glow breathes softly.
No animation; only a static tinted border.
projectSteward.aiSessionRunningCardAnima.description: Animation shown on OTHER WINDOWS project cards while AI sessions are executing in another window.

projectSteward.maxVisibleTodosPerGroup.markdownDescription: Maximum number of TODO items visible in each TODO group before the group list scrolls.

projectSteward.maxVisibleProjectsPerGrou.markdownDescription: Maximum number of project cards visible in each group on the Projects tab before the group list scrolls.

projectSteward.aiSessionAttention.enable.description: Show attention indicators when Project Steward AI sessions finish or may need input.

projectSteward.openOnStartup.markdownDescription: Note: The ```empty workspace``` option takes only effect if [Workbench: Startup Editor](#workbench.startupEditor) is to ```none``` or a ```welcomePage``` option.

projectSteward.showAddGroupButtonTile.markdownDescription: If set to ```false```, the '+ New Group' tile is hidden. Groups can be added by using the Command Palette (default ```F1```) with 'Project Steward: Add Group' action. The Project Steward needs to be refreshed for the changes to take effect.

projectSteward.applyProjectColorToWindow.markdownDescription: If set to ```true```, Project Steward applies the saved project color to the current VS Code window by writing workspace ```workbench.colorCustomizations``` values for title bar, activity bar, and status bar.

scripts.test:contract: npm run test-compile && npm run attention:bridge:compile && node --test --test-concurrency=1 'tests/contract/**/*.test.js'
scripts.test:integration: npm run test-compile && npm run attention:bridge:compile && node --test --test-concurrency=1 'tests/integration/**/*.test.js'
scripts.test:deterministic: npm run test:unit && npm run test:contract && npm run test:integration
scripts.test:open-projects: npm run test-compile && npm run attention:bridge:compile && node scripts/run-open-project-safety-checks.js
scripts.test:safety: npm run test-compile && npm run attention:bridge:compile && node scripts/run-ai-session-tmux-checks.js && node scripts/run-ai-session-safety-checks.js && node scripts/run-open-project-safety-checks.js
scripts.spike:attention:test: npm run spike:attention:compile && npm run attention:bridge:compile && node scripts/run-attention-local-bridge-spike-checks.js
scripts.attention:package: npm run spike:attention:test && npm run spike:attention:bundle && npm run attention:bridge:bundle && node scripts/package-attention-extensions.js
